titleIntro: The Museum of the Four Elements
resumeIntro: Through this exhibition, discover the raw power of nature's elements, from the summit of the highest peaks to the depths of the ocean. Here, you don't just read manga, you feel them.
Only one question remains, lingering: in the face of uncontrollable nature, is mankind still master of its own destiny?
Between contemplation and vertigo, be shaken by the vision of Japan's greatest authors. Will you find your own answer? The experience begins here.

1.title:
Nausicaä of
the Valley of the Wind
1.text: In the heart of a world where human steel has torn the sky apart, Nausicaa moves through a lost humanity. Prisoner not of chains but of mankind’s madness, she witnesses a war she does not understand. Why destroy when listening would be enough? Why hate when love could suffice? Guided by a fragile yet resilient will, Nausicaa refuses fatality.

2.title: The Summit of the Gods
2.text: They each pursued a different goal, and from this adventure they will emerge transformed — if they emerge alive. One seeks the truth behind the mountain’s mystery and the missing climbers. The other has lived only to reach the summit. Through this ascent, each must confront nature, their limits, and the obsession that drives some men ever higher… even if it means never coming down.

3.title: Children of the Sea
3.text: At the heart of a suspended summer, an unexpected encounter changes the course of days. Ruka meets two boys with ocean eyes, Umi and Sora. By their side, she slowly learns to listen to the sea, to breathe with it, to merge with it. The waves whisper a secret from the depths: we are made of the same matter as stars and oceans.

4.title:
Firefighter Daigo:
Rescuer in Orange
4.text: Flames destroyed his life. A survivor of a fire when he was still a child, he swore to become a firefighter to help others as he once was helped. But Daigo quickly faces reality when he understands that a single second of doubt can cost a life. The greater the danger, the more he is ready to risk his own. But beware of flying too close to the sun — before fire, man is nothing.

infoEnd: Last admission to the museum at 5:00 pm, last admission to the exhibitions at 5:15 pm, galleries begin closing from 5:30 pm.
infoThursday: Late opening on Thursdays until 9:45 pm. Last admission to the museum and exhibitions at 9:00 pm, galleries begin closing from 9:15 pm.
infoClosed: Closed every Monday, May 1st and December 25th.

victoryText: You survived your enemies.But was it really worth all the sacrifices? Discover the answer by reserving your place.
endText: Your enemies have shot you down, and your aircraft has crashed. Did you truly deserve to die this way? Discover the answer by reserving your place.
explainText: Your kingdom is under attack. You must flee, for only you can organize a counterattack. Avoid every enemy you encounter using your keyboard’s arrow keys.
